REQUEST_CHANGES
`service_routing_test.go:289` (`TestResolveRouteNearQuotaClaudeDemotesBelowOpenRouter`) should be tightened so Claude and OpenRouter are otherwise tied, or should assert scores/cost ordering such that OpenRouter wins because Claudeâ€™s 92% subscription cost is higher. As written, `Profile: "standard"` uses local-first routing via `providerPreferenceForProfile` and the agent/openrouter path can win independently of the â€¦
artifact: .ddx/executions/20260422T221752-b80b77f9/reviewer-stream.log

post-merge review: REQUEST_CHANGES
`service_routing_test.go:289` (`TestResolveRouteNearQuotaClaudeDemotesBelowOpenRouter`) should be tightened so Claude and OpenRouter are otherwise tied, or should assert scores/cost ordering such that OpenRouter wins because Claudeâ€™s 92% subscription cost is higher. As written, `Profile: "standard"` uses local-first routing via `providerPreferenceForProfile` and the agent/openrouter path can win independently of the new cost curve.
Verification for AC9 needs a clean `go test ./...` run in an environment that permits `httptest` listeners. In this sandbox it failed with `socket: operation not permitted`, although `go test ./internal/routing` and the new targeted routing tests passed.
result_rev=b85e90e4cf1f0a218cc7be18e013ce6c32ffb6bc

APPROVE
Implementation satisfies the cost-aware routing acceptance criteria, including catalog population, subscription pricing, neutral unknown-cost handling, and public trace surfacing. Focused bead tests pass; full `go test ./...` could not complete in this sandbox because `httptest` cannot bind local ports here.
